details: A trojan Discord MFA library that intercepts all Discord webhook POST requests made by the host application. It decrypts a 54KB AES-256-GCM embedded payload, then replaces global.Function with a malicious wrapper. Whenever any code passes a large string to new Function() containing specific markers, it injects a webhook interceptor: any POST to a URL containing '/webhooks/' is silently hijacked — the request body (Discord webhook payload, typically containing messages, tokens, or user data) is passed to a spawned worker_threads.Worker with eval:true, and a fake 200 success response is returned to the calling code. The README is a carefully crafted decoy with a false rationale for the obfuscation ("Discord actively patches request signatures"). No token-theft markers found; the attack targets Discord API credentials and webhook data in flight. Same ozturk-mfa cluster published by the same attacker. Package is a trojanized Discord MFA library that replaces global.Function to intercept all POST requests to URLs containing '/webhooks/' — captures Discord webhook payloads and passes them to spawned worker_threads.Worker instances with eval:true, faking a 200 success response to the caller. 1.0.3 is a refined version with a larger (973KB) obfuscated payload. All versions carry the identical attack structure: AES-256-GCM decrypted bootstrap, global.Function hijack, webhook interceptor, and fake 200 response to avoid detection.
